key_insight: This study releases a large, multicenter Swedish pediatric CNS tumor histopathology dataset to help train AI models for more accurate tumor detection and subtype classification.
clinical_relevance: By enabling better histology-based and multimodal analysis, the resource may support more precise diagnosis and improve pediatric brain tumor research and treatment planning.
method: Collected H&E whole-slide images from pediatric CNS tumor samples across all six Swedish pediatric oncology centers (2013–2023).
Curated 1,446 quality-controlled WSIs from 537 unique subjects with confirmed diagnoses.
Linked diagnostic clinical information, with matched WGS, WTS, and methylation data available through separate biobank resources for many cases.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports 1,446 sufficient-quality WSIs from 562 subject-diagnosis pairs, making this a substantial national cohort resource for AI and multimodal studies.
watch_item: As a curated retrospective dataset, performance may not generalize without external validation across scanners, sites, and rare tumor subtypes.
paper_title: A Multicenter Swedish Histopathology Image Dataset of Pediatric Central Nervous System Tumors
